_mode: great_hall on an Intel Iris Xe iGPU: same look as render.json, cut to fit the frame budget. Select with CLIENT_RENDER_CONFIG=datasets/great_hall_export/render_xe.json.
_gi_samples: 1024 on the discrete-GPU config; 8x fewer rays per probe solve measured as no material change in frame time, so the rays were never the bottleneck.
_gi_cadence: THE perf knob, and also a correctness one. render.json's 2/1 re-solves every probe every other frame with no staggering; the gi_bounce feedback then never settles to the same equilibrium it reaches on a faster machine, so the hall renders DARKER at the same settings. Back on the engine-default 8/2 cadence the probe solves per frame drop ~8x AND both machines converge to the same brightness.
_perf: 4x MSAA on every forward-pass fragment is the other big iGPU cost and buys little at this resolution.
_pagecache: rpg leak fix: sdf_resident_slots sizes the fine-chunk GPU pool (12 fits every gh chunk on a discrete GPU); sdf_fp16/lm_fp16 halve chunk textures + SH pages. The _zone.sdf coarse fallback covers any page fault.
